summary: DirectDine iter10 tested (backend + frontend). Backend: 12/12 iter10 tests PASS (5 new campaign-days scenarios + 7 iter9 regression) plus separate re-run of iter9 suite 11/11 PASS = 23/23 backend. Verified POST /api/restaurants/me/campaign/start accepts {days:7} → 200 with ends_at ~7d ahead (±1h), {days:14} → 200 with ends_at ~14d; /stop clears campaign_ends_at. {days:100} → 422 (le=90), {days:0} → 422 (ge=1). POST with NO body → 200 defaulting to 3 days. Iter9 regressions all green: owner /auth/me, PUT /restaurants/me min_order & radius, radius=0 → 422, GET /restaurants/{id}/menu display_price = price*(1+fee/100) with no campaign, POST /orders below min → 400 with 'Minimum order value', /admin/cleanup-junk-restaurants idempotent. Frontend desktop @1280x800: after owner1 login → sidebar (testID='owner-sidebar', width 240, height 800) visible with EasYum brand, Restaurant Admin subtitle, Marco Rossi user chip, sidebar-dashboard/menu/couriers/profile all clickable and navigate (URLs /menu, /couriers, /profile), sidebar-logout present, v1.1 · 0% commission footer shown. Content area fills remaining 1040px without overflow. Restaurant settings modal shows all five duration chips (campaign-duration-1/-3/-7/-14/-30). Clicking 7d starts campaign, modal then shows red 'Stop campaign' button and 'Ends 7/20/2026, 9:00:42 AM' text (~7 days after Jan 13). Stop button reverts to chips. Frontend mobile @390x844: owner-sidebar NOT rendered (as intended), bottom tabs Orders/Menu/Couriers/Profile all present — mobile layout unchanged. Deprecated packages detected: none new in iter10 scope. Only console noise: expo-notifications listener warning on web (pre-existing) and RN Web 'shadow*' & 'props.pointerEvents' deprecation warnings (pre-existing, cosmetic).

issues: On desktop the duration chip row is placed BELOW the Cancel/Save button row, which is visually awkward — user clicks Save first then sees the chips. Consider moving the ⚡ 'Pick campaign duration' section above the Save/Cancel actions, or at least visually grouping the campaign controls in their own labeled section (like RESTAURANT LOGO / DELIVERY RADIUS blocks) for better hierarchy.

action_items: (Optional cosmetic) Re-order Restaurant Settings modal so campaign duration chips + hint appear as a labeled section ABOVE the Save/Cancel buttons rather than trailing after them.

(Carry-over from iter9, still not fixed) Campaign strike-through discount price on customer restaurant screen — /app/frontend/app/(customer)/restaurant/[id].tsx line ~106-117 has inverted `hasDiscount = display_price < price` logic. Should compute original = price*(1+delivery_fee_pct/100) and strike THAT when campaign is active.
(Carry-over) server.py has grown to 1341 lines — consider splitting into routers/modules.
critical_code_review_comments: backend/server.py:509-525 CampaignStartInput with Field(default=3, ge=1, le=90) is correct; Body(default_factory=CampaignStartInput) correctly allows empty body → default 3. Validated by tests.
OwnerSidebar.tsx uses correct testIDs (owner-sidebar, sidebar-{dashboard|menu|couriers|profile}, sidebar-logout). Nav via router.push works.
useIsDesktop.ts correctly gates: Platform.OS === 'web' && width >= 900. Mobile web at 390px correctly returns false; sidebar hidden, tabBar visible.
(owner)/_layout.tsx uses tabBarStyle: { display: 'none' } on desktop to hide bottom tabs — clean approach.
profile.tsx renders duration chips only when !campaign_active and swaps to Stop-campaign + 'Ends …' when active — matches spec.
Carry-over: /app/frontend/app/(customer)/restaurant/[id].tsx line ~106 hasDiscount logic remains inverted from iter9.

success_rate.frontend: 100% for iter10 scope (desktop sidebar + campaign-duration + mobile regression); iter9 carry-over strike-through bug still open

seed_data_creation: Started/stopped campaigns on owner1's Bella Napoli during testing; each test toggles /campaign/stop via autouse fixture. Bella Napoli restored to min_order_value=0, delivery_radius_km=4 at teardown. No new restaurants/menu items/orders created (no orders placed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter10 backend contract is stable: POST /api/restaurants/me/campaign/start accepts optional {days:int (ge=1,le=90, default 3)}, returns Restaurant with campaign_active=true and ISO campaign_ends_at. Sidebar test-ids finalized as owner-sidebar / sidebar-{dashboard,menu,couriers,profile} / sidebar-logout. useIsDesktop breakpoint is 900px — anything <900 goes mobile. When testing owner desktop flows, remember chips are hidden while campaign is active; call /campaign/stop first to see chips again. Login testID is login-submit-button (with the -button suffix).
